paper_title: Polynomial bounds for chromatic number VII. Disjoint holes
summary: The conjecture that every forest H is good (i.e., H-free graphs are polynomially χ-bounded) remains fully open for general H-free graph classes. Partial progress exists: companion paper arXiv:2202.10412 (same 2022 series) proves compositional closure results (H good implies H∪P₄ good), and arXiv:2407.16882 (2024) proves the polynomial Gyárfás–Sumner conjecture holds for the restricted class of intersection graphs of axis-aligned boxes in ℝ^d with no induced forest subgraph. The full statement for arbitrary H-free graphs is unresolved.

since_posted.title: Polynomial Gyárfás-Sumner conjecture for graphs of bounded boxicity
since_posted.claim: Proves the polynomial Gyárfás–Sumner conjecture for intersection graphs of axis-aligned boxes in ℝ^d: for every forest F and positive integer d, such graphs with no induced F subgraph are polynomially χ-bounded.

internal_refs_verified.note: Confirmed as companion paper VI in the same 2022 series (same four authors, posted Feb 2022 concurrently). Proves that if H is good then H∪P₄ is good, and more generally that {H₁,H₂}-free graphs are polynomially χ-bounded when every component of H₁ is good and H₂ is a path or broom. This is a partial/compositional result; the full conjecture remains open. Because the arXiv ID (2202.xxxxx) places it in the same month as the source paper, it is not a strict follow-up and is excluded from since_posted.

Not fetched within the 5-call budget. Based on title ('Suns in triangle-free graphs of large chromatic number') and attribution to Trotignon, this paper appears to address a different conjecture about sun-free graphs and is likely off-topic for the polynomial Gyárfás–Sumner conjecture; inclusion in internal refs appears to be a fuzzy-match artifact.

WebFetch confirms this paper ('Induced subgraphs and tree decompositions XIV') proves that every clock-free graph of large treewidth contains a basic obstruction (complete graph, wall subdivision, or line graph of wall subdivision). It concerns treewidth and induced subgraph structure in clock-free graphs, not polynomial χ-boundedness for forests. The supplied contribution text ('Proves the conjecture in full') is mismatched to this conjecture; this reference appears three times in the internal refs, all with the same erroneous contribution text.

search_queries: polynomial chi-bounded H-free graphs forest Gyárfás-Sumner conjecture 2022 2023 2024 2025
notes: The polynomial Gyárfás–Sumner conjecture is an active open problem. Known good forests (as of early 2026) include paths up to P₄, some trees, and their disjoint unions under certain conditions, but P₅ was still open at the time of the source paper. The 2024 result (arXiv:2407.16882) restricts to bounded-boxicity graphs, not general H-free graphs. Esperet's conjecture that every χ-bounded class is polynomially χ-bounded has been disproved, making the polynomial Gyárfás–Sumner conjecture even more specific and harder.
